_article: XXXIV — Relationships Are Graphs. XXXIX.7 — the simulation queries a world database including a Relationship Graph, not raw JSON.
_description: Schema for the COMPILED Relationship Graph — the master graph of all directed, typed edges between entities, produced by the World Compiler from the DSL's relationship declarations (dsl_specification §3). This is the graph the simulation traverses to answer 'if the wolves disappear, what changes?' or 'who protects Wang Village?' or 'what does Wang Village depend on?'.

query_patterns: outgoing(entity_id, type?) -> [edge] — what does this entity relate to (optionally of a type)
incoming(entity_id, type?) -> [edge] — what relates TO this entity
cascade(entity_id, type, depth) -> subgraph — traverse N hops of a relationship type (e.g. 'what does Wang Village depend on, transitively?')
path(entity_a, entity_b, edge_type?) -> [edge] — is there a relationship path between two entities
_design_notes.sub_graphs: The Travel, Spirit, Hydrology, Settlement, and Influence graphs are PROJECTIONS of this master graph (filtered by edge type). They are compiled separately for O(1) queries in their domains, but they share entity ids with this graph.

nodes.compiled_state_ref: string — reference to full compiled state

edges.type: enum — from dsl_specification §3 (located_in, fed_by, protected_by, threatened_by, connected_to, drains_into, preys_on, governs, allied_with, at_war_with, owes, grudge_against, recruits_from, origin_of, spread_to, witnessed_by, known_by, ...)
edges.since: int — tick the relationship began (canon tick or historical event id)
edges.strength: float 0.0–1.0 — for soft relationships (allied_with, spread_to); omitted for hard structural relationships (located_in, drains_into)
edges.momentum_ref: string|null — reference to a live momentum in momentum_registry if the relationship itself has a trajectory (e.g. a decaying alliance, a festering grudge)
edges.properties: object — type-specific properties (e.g. a connected_to edge carries the RoadSegment id; a grudge_against edge carries the Grudge entity id)

indices.by_type: map<edge_type, [edge_id]> — for 'all threats', 'all supply routes', etc.

_compile_source: World Blueprint DSL v2 — all relationship declarations in entities' `relationships` blocks
_runtime_consumers: IntentEngine (who can help / who is in the way)
